data.title: From 100% Rebate to 20%: The Game Behind Polymarket's Fee Adjustment
data.description:
Author: DFarm Source: X, @DFarm_club

First, let's summarize the recent timeline of events related to Polymarket fees.

  1. Polymarket suddenly announced that it would charge fees for 15-minute cryptocurrency price prediction trades, but all fees collected would be refunded to the market makers (those placing limit orders).

  2. The fee refund was changed from full refund to partial refund.

  3. Until the 11th, the 100% fee refund stopped. From the 12th to the 18th, 20% of the fees were refunded.

  4. Why are transaction fees charged? We all know that Polymarket previously didn't charge any fees for basic transactions. Why is a fee being charged only for the 15-minute cryptocurrency market? This requires explaining what a "delayed arbitrage" bot is. In a very short market cycle like 15 minutes, the outcome is determined by the prices on various exchanges. Without transaction fees, high-frequency trading bots would exploit millisecond-level time differences to place orders and profit before Polymarket prices have had time to update. For example, if the probability of BTC rising (UP) in the 15-minute timeframe on Polymarket is currently 90%, and suddenly the price of BTC on the exchange drops by 5%, the bot immediately buys in at a lower price (DOWN). After that, other bots or traders follow suit, gradually buying to push the price up, allowing the bot to profit and exit. What are the consequences of this behavior? Market makers are constantly being exploited by these high-frequency bots, making them unwilling to continue providing liquidity in such markets, ultimately leading to decreased liquidity in the 15-minute cryptocurrency market.

    Therefore, the official platform introduced a transaction fee mechanism at this time, especially with the highest fees when the odds are 50:50 (as shown in the image). This directly caused the arbitrage costs of many bots to exceed their profits, and these bots naturally shut down.

    Why subsidize market makers?

    As mentioned earlier, market makers had previously had too much capital taken away. In order to retain market makers, the platform will share the transaction fees with those who place orders (market makers).

    So why was the subsidy reduced from 100% to 20%? The detail lies in this sentence: "From the 12th to the 18th, 20% of the transaction fee will be refunded." This tells us that the refund percentage after the 18th is pending. When the platform initially implemented transaction fees, market makers were unsure whether it could stop bots. That's why the platform refunded 100% of the transaction fees to market makers, covering their risk and maintaining liquidity. Why is only 20% being returned now? Let's look at the data first: After enabling transaction fees, the total transaction fees dropped by half. What does this mean? It means that many high-frequency trading bots have indeed shut down. Seeing that the bots have left and the market maker risk has decreased, the official response is that a 100% transaction fee return may not be necessary. They're offering 20% ​​first to see how the data performs. This is why they're piloting a 20% transaction fee return for a week first, observing the data performance, and then determining the subsequent return percentage. In fact, all of this is about balancing the interests of market makers, bots, and ordinary traders. "Money Printing Machine" Bots Polymarket has too many "money printing machines" in its market, and very few people truly know how they do it. The most popular post is probably an article by X user @the_smart_ape: [Image link: https://uploads.panewslab.com/2026z/69246520-265f-4add-873a-053429ec66c2](https://uploads.panewslab.com/2026z/69246520-265f-4add-873a-053429ec66c2) ... No, if you can crack their strategy, your "money-printing machine" won't be far off. But remember, don't tell anyone else, though you can tell me secretly. Finally, on Polymarket, since there are no third-party commissions or fees, we're essentially betting against each other. Therefore, as a platform, their responsibility is to provide a fair opportunity for both sides to compete. Players who enjoy PVP games know that absolute fairness doesn't exist; it can only be achieved through iterations version by version, striving for the best possible fairness. This also shows us that a "money-printing machine" does indeed exist on Polymarket, where success is built on technology and strategy.

data.title: Golden Web3.0 Daily Report | Visa and BVNK Launch Stablecoin Payment Functionality
data.description:
DeFi data

1. Total market value of DeFi tokens: $111.158 billion

DeFi Total Market Cap Data Source: coingecko

2. Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours: $40.81

Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours. Data source: coingecko

3. Assets locked in DeFi: $124.549 billion$124.549 billion

Top 10 DeFi Projects by Locked Assets and Total Value Locked. Data Source: defillama

1. Total Market Value of NFTs: $114.657 billion

Top 10 NFTs by Sales Growth in 24 Hours Data Source: NFTGO

Headlines

Visa Partners with BVNK to Launch Stablecoin Payment Function

Visa is partnering with BVNK to launch stablecoin payment functionality

Visa is currently integrating BVNK with BVNK's stablecoin payment functionality

The stablecoin infrastructure has been integrated into its Visa Direct platform, expanding the way its $1.7 trillion real-time payments network transfers funds globally. The partnership, announced Wednesday, allows businesses in select markets to prepay in stablecoins and have the funds sent directly to the recipient's digital wallet.

      MEME Hot Topics

      1. Changpeng Zhao: True Meme Coin Projects Need to Have "Historicity" and "Legendary Significance"

      According to Jinse Finance, Changpeng Zhao stated in a Binance Square AMA livestream: "Early investors in Meme Coin also face certain risks, and subsequent buyers are conducting their own research. This phenomenon does not require third-party intervention for the time being. True Meme Coin projects need to have historical significance and 'legacy,' but only a very small number truly have value, with a failure rate exceeding 90%. Investors need to be responsible for their choices." 2. "Binance Life" breaks through $0.285, with a 24-hour increase of 58.95%. According to Jinse Finance, market data shows that "Binance Life" topped the Binance gainers list, breaking through $0.285 and currently trading at $0.2712, a 24-hour increase of 58.95%. Market volatility is high; please manage your risk. DeFi Hot Topics 1. Vitalik publishes an article reviewing his 2014 blockchain vision, stating that a decentralized revival is imminent. According to Jinse Finance, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik... Buterin, reflecting on his 2014 vision for blockchain on social media, envisioned permissionless decentralized applications supporting finance, social media, ride-sharing, organizational governance, crowdfunding, and more, potentially creating a completely different alternative network, all built on a single technology. Over the past five years, this core vision has sometimes become blurred, with various "meta-narratives" and "themes" dominating the landscape. But the core vision has never died. In fact, the core technologies underpinning this vision are becoming increasingly powerful. Vitalik stated that in 2014, decentralized applications were just toys, hundreds of times more difficult to use in the Web 2.0 era. By 2026, Fileverse will be usable enough for me to frequently write documents and send them to others for collaboration. A decentralized revival is coming, and you too can be a part of it. 2. ETHGas Announces Governance Token GWEI Jinse Finance reports that the ETHGas Foundation has announced the launch of its governance token, GWEI, to manage the "Real-Time Ethereum" infrastructure, aiming to alleviate latency and volatility caused by blind competition in the block space. Details of TGE will be released soon, with a snapshot scheduled for January 19th at 00:00 UTC. ETHGas completed a $12 million token funding round last month, led by Polychain Capital. 3. BNB Chain Undergoes Fermi Upgrade Today, Boosting Block Production Speed ​​by 40% Jinse Finance reports that BNB Chain today underwent its third major upgrade, Fermi, named after the Italian-American physicist Enrico Fermi. The upgrade will increase network block production speed by 40% and reduce final confirmation time to approximately 1 second, making transaction confirmation more predictable and reducing slippage risk in highly volatile markets. This upgrade also includes technical improvements such as optimizing the validator coordination mechanism, improving blockchain data access efficiency, and enhancing transaction execution efficiency. Previously, BNB Chain completed two upgrades named after mathematicians Pascal and Maxwell.

      4. Solana Treasury Upexi Reaches Securities Purchase Agreement to Raise $36 Million

      According to Jinse Finance, Nasdaq-listed Solana Treasury Upexi announced a securities purchase agreement with Hivemind Capital Partners to raise $36 million. The note issuance is expected to be completed around January 14, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. The notes are backed by locked SOL tokens and will be incorporated into the company's Solana treasury upon completion of the transaction. This transaction is expected to increase Upexi's SOL holdings by 12% to over 2.4 million SOL, making it the second-largest corporate SOL holder after Forward Industries, which holds 6.9 million SOL.

        5.CZ Clarifies: Genius is not a competitor of Aster, but a Perp DEX trading terminal

        According to Jinse Finance, Binance founder CZ commented on a social media discussion regarding the "competitive relationship between CZ's new advisory project, Genius Trading, and Aster," stating, "Genius is a trading terminal connecting the perpetual contract decentralized exchange (Perp DEX), not a competitor of Aster." Yesterday's report indicated that YZi Labs had made a "tens of millions of dollars (multi-8-figure)" investment in Genius Trading, and CZ would simultaneously join the project as an advisor. Genius co-founder and CEO Armaan Kalsi revealed that YZi Labs' investment occurred last month and was significantly higher than $10 million.

        He declined to disclose the specific size and investment structure (equity, token, or hybrid form), nor did he respond to whether Genius plans to issue its own token. Disclaimer: Jinse Finance, as a blockchain information platform, publishes articles for informational purposes only and does not constitute actual investment advice. Please establish sound investment principles and be sure to enhance your risk awareness.

data.title: The Ghost of X.com: Musk's 25-Year Revenge
data.description:
Author: Elon Musk, Deep Tide TechFlow

Some dreams don't die; they just wait for their time.

The premature baby of 1999

In March 1999, in Palo Alto, 27-year-old Elon Musk made a decision that seemed almost absurd at the time.

He bet all $22 million he earned from selling Zip2 on a website called X.com.

At that time, Silicon Valley was still the era of Yahoo and AOL; people equated the internet with portal websites. Proposing the concept of "online banking" at this point was like trying to sell rockets in the age of horse-drawn carriages.

But Musk's vision for X.com wasn't just an online bank; he wanted to create an online financial operating system: a platform that would handle all financial services—transfers, investments, loans, insurance, and even everyday spending. At the time, Silicon Valley thought this young man from South Africa was crazy. It was the dial-up era, with modems blaring and web pages sometimes taking half a minute to load. Letting users transfer money on a snail's pace of 28.8K? It sounded like a joke. His ambition was terrifying, but reality dealt him a harsher blow. A year later, X.com merged with Peter Thiel's Confinity (the predecessor to PayPal). What should have been a "golden partnership" turned into a Silicon Valley version of Game of Thrones. Thiel and his Stanford elites disapproved of Musk's haphazard and radical approach, considering this engineer-turned-CEO a dangerous madman. In September 2000, the collapse occurred. Musk flew to Australia for his honeymoon. Just as his flight landed in Sydney, before he even stepped out of the airport, the board called: You're out. Peter Thiel took over everything. A few months later, Musk's beloved "X.com" sign was torn down, and the company was renamed PayPal. The foundation of Musk's "financial empire," built over a year, was razed by a group of investment bankers in custom Brioni suits, leaving only the simplest function: payments. In 2002, eBay acquired PayPal, and Musk received $180 million. He won in terms of wealth, but in that moment, he felt like a child whose beloved toy had been snatched away. A fishbone pierced his heart. For the next twenty years, he built the best electric car, sent rockets into space, and vowed to die on Mars. But whenever PayPal was mentioned, he couldn't hide his dejection. X.com remained his inner demon. Bringing a "washbasin" to Wall Street On October 27, 2022, Musk walked into Twitter headquarters carrying a washbasin. This detail was later widely reported by the media, but the real signal was his tweet: "Let that sink in." A pun. Let the sink in, and let everything settle down. The outside world thought he bought Twitter for freedom of speech, or to seek justice for Trump. They were wrong. Musk wanted revenge, revenge for the betrayal 25 years ago. The first step was changing his name. X. One letter, carrying all his anger and ambition. Those who mocked X.com for being too ahead of its time are now witnessing its revival on this platform. But Musk is smart. He knows he can't do it all at once; turning Twitter directly into a bank would scare users away. So he chose a gradual transformation. In early 2023, X was primarily a lightweight social platform with a 140-character limit. Musk first adjusted the content strategy, encouraging more original content and real-time discussions. Then came paid subscriptions, getting users used to spending money on the platform. By mid-year, the long tweet feature was launched. Users could post longer, more in-depth content, and the platform began to transform from a short message square into an information hub. Next came a significant enhancement to the video functionality. Musk wanted X to be a one-stop platform for information consumption, eliminating the need for users to switch to YouTube or other video websites. In late 2023, the creator revenue-sharing program was officially launched. The platform began to have an economic ecosystem, allowing users to earn income through content creation. This was a crucial step; Musk was cultivating users' trading habits. Then came the big moves in 2024. Financial license applications, payment system construction… Musk was no longer hiding anything; he wanted to turn X into a financial platform. In January 2026, Nikita Bier, head of X products, stated that the platform was developing the Smart Cashtags feature, allowing users to precisely point to specific assets or smart contracts when publishing market data. Users can embed hashtags like $TSLA in tweets to display the stock price in real time. What seems like just an information display feature is actually the final piece of the financialization puzzle. Imagine: you see a news item on X about Nvidia's new chip, the stock price immediately jumps 5%, and you directly click the $NVDA hashtag to place a buy order. Social, information, and trading—a trinity—this is the vision Musk wanted to achieve at X.com. From city squares to information centers, and then to trading floors, Musk spent two years gradually guiding users to accept X's transformation. To allay user concerns, Musk made an unprecedented decision: to open-source all algorithms. On January 10, 2026, Musk announced on X that he would officially open-source the latest content recommendation algorithm for the X platform within a week, covering both organic and advertising content code, with updates every four weeks including developer notes. The recommendation algorithms of platforms like Facebook, YouTube, and TikTok are black boxes; no one knows why they see certain content. This lack of transparency becomes a fatal flaw when it comes to financial services. Musk broke the black box with open source. Users can inspect the code, developers can audit security, and regulators can oversee compliance. All of this paves the way for financialization. The Belated Verification: X.com in 1999 died because it was "born at the wrong time." Back then, the internet was still dial-up, broadband penetration was less than 10%, online payments required over a dozen security verifications, and users were terrified of putting their money online. More importantly, the regulatory environment was extremely stringent. Banking regulators viewed internet finance as a dangerous threat, and the government was also cautiously navigating the system. Musk's aggressive strategy seemed too risky in that conservative era. But history proved him right. The verification simply came too late, and from an unexpected place: China. In 2011, WeChat was launched. Initially just a chat application, it quickly became the super app Musk had envisioned. Chatting, paying, hailing rides, ordering food, managing finances—it can do everything. Alipay has evolved from a simple third-party payment platform into a comprehensive financial platform. Musk saw this and was anxious. In June 2022, at his first all-hands meeting with Twitter employees, he publicly stated, "In China, people basically live on WeChat because it's so practical and helpful for daily life. I think if we can reach that level on Twitter, or even just get close to it, it will be a huge success." This sounds like praise for WeChat, but also regret for his failure 25 years ago. The Chinese have achieved in ten years what he wanted to do back in 1999. Now it's his turn. Mobile payments have rewritten the consumption habits of users worldwide, and cryptocurrency has transformed from a geek toy into an investment target for pension funds. Blockchain technology has made decentralized finance a reality. Regulators are also embracing innovation. The SEC has approved a Bitcoin ETF, the EU has launched a digital euro plan, and the People's Bank of China is piloting a digital yuan. Musk has waited 25 years for this opportunity. With this background, looking at Smart Cashtags, you'll understand that Musk's opponent has never been Zuckerberg. Meta controls social relationships, Google controls information indexing, and Apple controls hardware entry points. But so far, no tech giant has truly controlled the global flow of funds. This is the ultimate outcome of X. Finance is the underlying protocol of the business world. Whoever controls the flow of funds holds the throat of the digital economy. This is far more damaging than creating a search engine or selling a mobile phone. Musk is reshaping a high-speed chain from "information" to "decision-making" to "action." Imagine this: Musk tweets about a new Tesla technology. Within seconds, hundreds of thousands of people click on the $TSLA hashtag. Algorithms predict trends based on sentiment analysis, automatically push trading suggestions, and users place orders with a single click. Influence instantly translates into trading volume. This is the financialization of social media. The traditional Wall Street model—analysts writing research reports and brokers making phone calls—will appear cumbersome and expensive in the face of algorithms. Returning to the initial question, why did Musk acquire Twitter? The answer has long been public. On October 5, 2022, Musk tweeted that acquiring Twitter accelerated the creation of the super app "X." Only now do people truly understand this statement.

Back in 1999, the ghost of X.com finally had its chance to be resurrected. This time, no one could stop him; he was no longer the 27-year-old entrepreneur who had to rely on others, but the world's richest man with absolute power.

Welcome to the X Universe

If we zoom out, stepping outside the ups and downs of Wall Street and the feuds of Silicon Valley, you will discover an even more chilling pattern.

Musk's obsession with the letter "X" has long transcended the realm of commercial branding, becoming an almost pathological totem worship.

Look at what he's done over the past twenty years: When he tried to send humans to Mars, he named the company SpaceX; when he wanted to build a flagship SUV that would define Tesla's future, he insisted on calling it Model X despite resistance; when he left OpenAI to develop his own large-scale artificial intelligence model, he named it xAI. He even named his most beloved son X A-12, but in daily life, he only calls him "Little X." In mathematics, X represents an unknown, representing infinite possibilities. But in Musk's life story, X is the only constant. 25 years ago, the young man ousted from PayPal lost his X. 25 years later, the richest man in the world, possessing rockets, cars, AI, and the world's largest media sphere, has finally retrieved that piece of the puzzle. Everything is for X to happen. Welcome to Musk's X universe.

data.title: 278 pages, 137 revisions: Crypto industry focuses on Senate's new banking bill
data.description:
Author: Eleanor Terrett Source: Crypto in America Translation: Shan Ouba, Jinse Finance

Editor's Note: The Senate's new banking bill proposes 137 amendments to the existing framework, with many provisions directly addressing the boundaries of cryptocurrency, stablecoin, and bank participation. The Senate Banking Committee's Market Structure Bill is about to be reviewed; this article analyzes the current progress. On Tuesday, the crypto industry was engrossed in studying the 278-page bill submitted by the Senate Banking Committee Monday evening—the culmination of five months of bipartisan negotiations. This was the first time most stakeholders had seen the complete bipartisan text, and reactions were relatively restrained, with no overwhelming criticism or praise. Most stakeholders indicated they would wait for the details of the senators' proposed amendments to be finalized before making a final judgment, with some amendments scheduled for a vote on Thursday. These details touch upon the core issues that have sparked controversy in recent weeks: DeFi, with the unexpected addition of a new chapter raising industry concerns; stablecoin yields – many crypto industry participants say they are already precarious; ethical provisions – details remain unclear, and the White House and Democrats have not yet reached a consensus; and a new point of concern – whether the wording related to tokenization is biased towards traditional finance. "There are many things in the bill that worry us. We need to clarify the scope of the amendments and their potential impact before we can truly assess the situation we will face," a DeFi industry leader told Crypto In America. Yesterday afternoon at 5 pm was the deadline for submitting amendments, and members of the Senate Banking Committee ultimately proposed 137 independent amendments. According to a list reviewed by Crypto In America, Maryland Democratic Senator Angela Alsobrooks and North Carolina Republican Senator Thom Tillis jointly proposed several bipartisan amendments to further refine which compliant activities can allow users to earn stablecoin yields. Nebraska Republican Senator Pete Ricketts and Wyoming Republican Senator Cynthia Lummis proposed amendments to DeFi-related wording. Maryland Democratic Senator Chris Van Hollen proposed ethical provisions aimed at “preventing cryptocurrency corruption,” including prohibiting government officials from profiting from crypto companies, and an “anti-sales” amendment requiring individuals to disclose their financial interests when posting crypto-related advertisements. Due to the Senate Banking Committee’s limited jurisdiction in ethical matters, these provisions, despite widespread support from Democrats on the committee, are unlikely to be voted on in this round of deliberations. Massachusetts Senator Elizabeth Warren, a well-known crypto skeptic, introduced more than 20 amendments, including banning stablecoins from paying yield and rescinding the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency's (OCC) crypto-friendly guidance issued last year. Some proposals are not directly related to core crypto legislation; for example, an amendment proposed by Rhode Island Democratic Senator Jack Reed seeks the committee's support for Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell during the Justice Department's criminal investigation. Such proposals suggest that some committee Democrats who are not interested in crypto issues may use this deliberation to advance broader partisan positions. As Patrick Witt, executive director of the White House Crypto Committee, revealed to Crypto In America on Monday, the ethics clauses, once finalized, will be added before the bill passes committee deliberation and is submitted to the full Senate for a vote, meaning Thursday's deliberation was just the beginning of the legislative process. Last night, Senator John Boozman, Chairman of the Senate Agriculture Committee and a Republican from Arkansas, announced that the committee would reschedule its deliberations on the bill to 3 p.m. on January 27, with the text of the bill to be released a week prior.

data.title: Where did Yao get his 2000 Ethereum? How did he use them?
data.description: Tonight at 8 PM, I put aside my other work and watched the entire anti-corruption documentary "Technology Empowering Anti-Corruption." Since leaving the discipline inspection and supervision system in 2019, because a large part of my work involves criminal defense in the field of virtual digital currency, I have been paying close attention to relevant reports from the discipline inspection system. Previously, the most influential cases were those of Xiao Yi, the former secretary of the Fuzhou Municipal Committee of Jiangxi Province (the former secretary who supported cryptocurrency mining), who was sentenced but left us with many questions, and Hao Gang, the former member of the Party Committee and deputy director of the Beijing Municipal Bureau of Local Financial Supervision and Administration (Beijing Municipal Bureau of Financial Work), whose self-laundering of money and demotion punishment were more detailed than before. This report on Yao Qian is more detailed than previous ones. The documentary mentions that there are no exceptions overseas, and that overseas is not above the law. It states that virtual digital currencies are "a new vehicle for corruption that must be guarded against," and characterizes Yao Qian's bribery case as "a new type of corruption, hidden corruption." The documentary reports that Yao Qian's bribe-givers were mainly two people: one was Wang, who worked in the traditional securities industry, and the other was Zhang, who was involved in virtual digital currency ICOs. In 2018, Zhang asked Yao Qian to endorse his ICO. Yao Qian contacted a virtual digital currency exchange, which raised 20,000 Ethereum for Zhang. Subsequently, Zhang gave 2,000 Ethereum to Yao Qian as a kickback. During Yao Qian's bribery process, a key "pawn" was Jiang Guoqing, his subordinate, who "participated in almost every large transaction Yao Qian made." Jiang Guoqing instructed Zhang to first transfer virtual currency to a transit address, and then he would operate to transfer it into a hardware wallet address controlled by Yao Qian. Disciplinary inspection and supervision personnel found the hardware wallet involved in the case in Yao Qian's office drawer. In 2021, Yao Qian used multiple alias accounts to sell 370 Ethereum, exchanging them for 10 million RMB to purchase a villa. The villa was registered under the name of a relative of Yao Qian. Disciplinary and supervisory personnel conducted a four-layer investigation, ultimately reconstructing the evidence chain showing Yao Qian used the proceeds from selling Ethereum to pay for the villa. As one investigator in the documentary put it, once virtual currency is used in the real world, it will inevitably be revealed. The villa was the "developer" that revealed Yao Qian's bribery. Previously, lawyer Liu Yang had also heard of numerous cases of bribery using virtual currency across various regions and had written an article on "The Determination of the Amount of Virtual Currency Bribes and the Investigative Approach." After watching the documentary on Yao Qian's case, the following situations or questions arise, and it is hoped that answers can be found in subsequent case reports: First, the documentary explicitly mentions that Yao Qian received Ethereum worth up to 60 million RMB. This is likely the first time the disciplinary inspection and supervision system has confirmed the asset value of virtual currency. Whether the final amount of Yao Qian's bribes will be determined based on the value of the virtual currency, or only the amount converted into cash, remains to be seen. Second, for some reason, Yao Qian's screen time in the documentary is extremely limited, consisting of only one shot and one sentence. Third, according to the documentary, Yao Qian did not convert all the virtual currency into cash. How to handle the virtual digital currency involved in the case is a valuable example for the legal community to study and consider in handling such cases. Fourth, whether Yao Qian's acceptance and repeated transfer of virtual currency, and his subsequent conversion into cash, constitutes "money laundering" remains to be answered by the judgment.

data.title: Details of the case involving Yao Qian, former director of the China Securities Regulatory Commission, who accepted $60 million in bribes in the cryptocurrency market, have been disclosed.
data.description:
Source: Website of the Central Commission for Discipline Inspection and the National Supervisory Commission, CCTV News

On the evening of January 14,the fourth episode of the TV documentary "Never Stop, Never Back Down," titled "Technology Empowering Anti-Corruption," was broadcast.

It analyzed the case of Yao Qian, the former director of the Technology Supervision Department of the China Securities Regulatory Commission.

The following is a partial transcript:

In addition to the spatial isolation between domestic and foreign countries, the physical isolation between online and offline has also become a means for corrupt officials to attempt to cover up their corrupt activities.

Gold, cash, and valuables are common vehicles for power-for-money transactions in traditional corruption cases. However, with the advent of the digital age and the continuous development of virtual currencies based on blockchain technology, new forms of corruption have emerged that require vigilance. These are key pieces of evidence seized in a case involving violations of discipline and law by leading cadres. Some look like mobile phones, some like USB drives, and some like remote controls. In fact, they are all different types of hardware wallets used to store and manage virtual currencies. The virtual currencies stored in these three seemingly inconspicuous little "wallets" are valued at tens of millions of yuan. Those who accept bribes in this way also harbored illusions, believing they were discreet enough.

Yao Qian, former director of the Science and Technology Supervision Department of the China Securities Regulatory Commission:To be honest, you knew it was a clandestine act, so how could you do it? It's just that you previously thought it would be difficult to find evidence. ...>

Zou Rong, staff member of the Discipline Inspection and Supervision Group of the Central Commission for Discipline Inspection and the National Supervisory Commission stationed at the China Securities Regulatory Commission:Supervision of this person requires profiling. He has a relatively long history of working in the digital currency field. Does this also imply corruption involving the use of virtual currencies for power-for-money transactions? In the practice of anti-corruption in the capital market, new types of corruption and hidden corruption are particularly prominent.

Zou Rong, staff member of the Discipline Inspection and Supervision Group of the Central Commission for Discipline Inspection and the National Supervisory Commission stationed at the China Securities Regulatory Commission:Supervision of this person requires profiling. He has a relatively long history of working in the digital currency field. Does this also imply corruption involving the use of virtual currencies for power-for-money transactions?In the practice of anti-corruption in the capital market, new types of corruption and hidden corruption are particularly prominent.

Cai Kunting, staff member of the Discipline Inspection and Supervision Commission of Shanwei City, Guangdong Province:A search requires knowing two things: first, whether there is a hardware wallet; second, whether there are notes with random mnemonic phrases written on them. These are crucial in a search.

Sure enough, the task force found a hardware wallet in a drawer in Yao Qian's office.

Meanwhile, the task force strictly adhered to regulations and laws, making full use of big data and information technology to conduct a comprehensive investigation into Yao Qian's situation, uncovering relevant clues. While legally accessing account information revealed no obvious abnormalities in Yao Qian's own accounts, cross-checking with big data revealed several bank accounts opened under other people's identities—actually Yao Qian's "shell accounts," controlled by him. Tracing the large sums of money entering and leaving these shell accounts led to a significant discovery: a single transaction of 10 million yuan.

Zou Rong：During our initial investigation, we discovered an unusual transfer of 10 million yuan into Yao Qian's account. After approximately four layers of investigation, we finally confirmed that it likely originated from a cryptocurrency exchange's account.

Zou Rong：We also discovered an unusual transfer of 10 million yuan into Yao Qian's account during our initial investigation. After approximately four layers of investigation, we finally confirmed that it likely originated from a cryptocurrency exchange's account.

... The investigation revealed that shortly after the 10 million yuan arrived in Yao Qian's "shell account," it was used to pay part of the purchase price for a villa in Beijing. This villa, valued at over 20 million yuan, was registered under the name of a relative of Yao Qian, but was actually owned by Yao Qian. All the funds for the purchase came from Yao Qian's "shell account." In addition to this 10 million yuan, two other large sums totaling 12 million yuan were also used to pay for properties. Tracing the source of this 12 million yuan revealed an equally complex origin.

Shi Changping, staff member of the Discipline Inspection and Supervision Commission of Shanwei City, Guangdong Province:He believed that after setting up multiple layers, it might be more isolated. But conversely, in the future, there would be more people and more sufficient evidence to prove the problem or the existence of the problem.

The special investigation team penetrated the layers of "smoke and mirrors," conducted in-depth investigations and collected evidence, and locked down the source of the 12 million yuan.

The money all came from an information service company controlled by businessman Wang. Further investigation revealed that Yao Qian had used his position to assist the company in providing technology services in the securities and futures industry, forming a complete chain of evidence linking this 12 million yuan in illicit gains. Wang also confessed to having a key intermediary in this bribery scheme named Jiang Guoqing, Yao Qian's subordinate, with whom he had a very close relationship. The special investigation team immediately placed Jiang Guoqing under detention. The investigation revealed that he was involved in almost every large-scale bribery transaction by Yao Qian, especially when Yao Qian accepted bribes in virtual currency.

Involved Person Jiang Guoqing:Originally, they wanted to transfer the money through me, but after thinking about it, I was afraid of causing trouble, so I set up a transfer address. Then, people would send the coins to the transfer address, and through the transfer address, they would be transferred to Yao Qian's personal wallet. I know this is a transfer of benefits, and I'm afraid of it. I also know this is wrong.

Involved Person Jiang Guoqing:Originally, they wanted to transfer the money through me, but after thinking about it, I was afraid of causing trouble, so I set up a transfer address. Then, they would send the coins to the transfer address, and through the transfer address, they would be transferred to Yao Qian's personal wallet. I know this is a transfer of benefits, and I'm afraid of it. I also know this is wrong.

data.title: Details of the case involving Yao Qian, former director of the China Securities Regulatory Commission, who accepted $60 million in bribes in the cryptocurrency market, have been disclosed.
data.description: Hello everyone, this is Good Things Guy. Tonight at 8 PM, the fourth episode of the TV documentary "Never Stop, Never Back Down," titled "Technology Empowering Anti-Corruption," will air on CCTV, revealing details of the corruption of Yao Qian, the former director of the Science and Technology Supervision Department of the China Securities Regulatory Commission, who accepted bribes using virtual currency. This former fintech expert thought he had found an "invisible channel" for corruption, but ultimately his true colors were exposed in the face of technology-driven anti-corruption efforts. "To be honest, I knew it was a clandestine act, so how could I do it? It's just that I thought it would be difficult to find evidence," Yao Qian confessed on camera. As the former director of the Digital Currency Research Institute of the People's Bank of China, Yao Qian was a leading expert in the field of fintech. However, he knowingly broke the law, using his in-depth understanding of virtual currencies for personal gain. The documentary showcased three seemingly ordinary small devices: one resembling a mobile phone, another a USB flash drive, and the third a remote control. These were Yao Qian's hardware wallets for storing and managing virtual currencies, with a total value equivalent to tens of millions of yuan. Yao Qian betrayed his original aspirations and mission, disregarded politics, and craved empty fame. Positioning himself as a fintech expert, he spared no effort in supporting specific technology service providers for personal gain, willingly becoming a "key target" for those seeking to exploit him. He abused his regulatory powers, including the power to advise, formulate, and implement policies, and abandoned his responsibilities for science and technology supervision, seeking improper benefits for others in areas such as expanding business and procuring software and hardware for information technology system service organizations. The "Invisibility" and "X-ray Vision" of Virtual Currency Corruption Virtual currencies, relying on blockchain technology, have become a new means for corrupt officials to conceal their corrupt activities. Unlike physical assets such as cash and gold, virtual currencies are generated based on cryptographic principles and are merely a string of digital symbols on the network. They are isolated from the existing account systems of commercial banks and payment institutions, completely circumventing national foreign exchange controls, and can be freely exchanged across borders. They are extremely concealed, difficult to detect, difficult to obtain evidence of, and difficult to solidify. However, by studying a great deal of professional knowledge and gaining a deep understanding of the operating mechanism of virtual currencies, the task force identified the key points for investigation. Investigators explained, "Everyone mainly controls the virtual currencies on the blockchain address using a private key. This private key consists of a string of characters dozens of characters long, and is usually stored in a hardware wallet." The task force strictly followed regulations and laws, making full use of big data and information technology to conduct a comprehensive investigation into Yao Qian's situation. The investigation found that although Yao Qian's personal account showed no obvious abnormalities, cross-checking using big data revealed that several bank accounts opened under other people's identities were actually Yao Qian's "front accounts," which he actually controlled. By tracing the large sums of money flowing in and out of these shell accounts, one transaction of 10 million yuan, after four layers of investigation, was ultimately confirmed to have originated from a cryptocurrency trader's account. Shortly after this 10 million yuan entered Yao Qian's shell account, it was used to pay part of the purchase price for a villa in Beijing. The investigation also revealed that Yao Qian had a corrupt relationship with an information service company controlled by businessman Wang, involving a sum as high as 12 million yuan. The special task force penetrated layers of deception to conduct a thorough investigation and gather evidence, pinpointing the source of these funds and forming a complete chain of evidence. The Downfall of the Corrupt "Clique" In the Yao Qian case, a key figure emerged—Jiang Guoqing, Yao Qian's subordinate, a trusted confidant, and a pawn in Yao Qian's path of corruption. Jiang Guoqing was involved in almost every large-scale power-for-money transaction by Yao Qian, especially when Yao Qian accepted bribes in the form of virtual currency. In 2018, a cryptocurrency boss surnamed Zhang asked Yao Qian through Jiang Guoqing to help his company with its token issuance and financing project. Yao Qian accepted the request, contacted a virtual currency exchange, and helped Zhang's company successfully issue tokens. Subsequently, Zhang gave Yao Qian 2,000 Ethereum as a token of gratitude. At its peak, the market value of these Ethereum exceeded 60 million yuan. The special investigation team utilized the publicly verifiable nature of blockchain technology to trace the complete flow of the 2,000 Ethereum from Zhang's wallet address to Yao Qian's wallet address, achieving mutual corroboration of various pieces of evidence and forming a closed loop. While rigorously investigating Yao Qian's corruption, the disciplinary inspection and supervision team stationed at the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) also conducted a systemic rectification campaign, continuously strengthening the atmosphere of strict discipline and focusing on preventing corruption risks. In response to the problems exposed by the case, the Party Committee of the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) improved the overall control mechanism for information system construction, including planning, review and approval, budget management, daily supervision, and performance evaluation, and formulated and revised more than 10 regulations. The ultimate fate of corrupt officials: In November 2024, Yao Qian was expelled from the Party and dismissed from public office. His suspected crimes were transferred to the procuratorate for review and prosecution, and the related assets were also transferred. Yao Qian seriously violated the Party's political discipline, the spirit of the Central Eight-Point Regulation, organizational discipline, and integrity discipline, constituting serious job-related violations and suspected bribery crimes. Moreover, he continued his misconduct even after the 18th, 19th, and even 20th National Congresses of the Communist Party of China, demonstrating a serious nature and egregious impact.

data.title: What if Powell chooses to stay and continue the hardline stance?
data.description:
01

Powell is stepping down as chairman in May, so why launch a criminal investigation at this time?

Because he was disobedient, because he was too slow, because he didn't cut interest rates fast enough, and because if he remained on the board, Trump would lose an opportunity to appoint his own people.

But it may not be entirely about him, but rather setting a rule for everyone—other members of the Federal Reserve Board, and officials throughout the federal government—to see what happens if they don't obey.

But it may not be entirely about him, but rather setting a rule for everyone—other members of the Federal Reserve Board, and officials throughout the federal government—to see what happens if they don't obey.

In any case, this is no longer just a matter of central bank independence, nor is it something as mild and innocuous as interfering in interest rate decisions. It has become an act of threatening "political opponents." The political term "terror" (French: terreur), originating from the Reign of Terror during the French Revolution, refers to the persecution of dissidents. Such behavior is not uncommon in politics, but its use against a central bank governor is truly unprecedented in American history. However, I myself was somewhat surprised by Mr. Powell's response. Throughout his term, Powell deliberately avoided political issues and did not respond to pressure from Trump. He could have easily dodged the issue this time, but he didn't; instead, he issued a direct video statement saying, "This is because we didn't set interest rates according to the president's wishes." Completely unvarnished and very direct. His words are often firm as he prepares to leave. This makes one somewhat more inclined to admire him. So, the question arises: Powell's term as chairman expires in May, but his term as a governor doesn't end until January 2028. Conventionally, a chairman usually leaves the council after leaving office. However, he can choose not to leave. What if he chooses to stay and continue his hardline stance? Polymarket immediately picked up on this possibility—before the announcement, the probability of Powell leaving the Federal Reserve Board by the end of the year was around 85%; after the announcement, it immediately dropped to 75%. This indicates that the market is already reassessing—the court subpoena was the final straw for Powell, and it's possible he'll stick to his guns and hold onto his board seat until 2028. This has historical precedent. In 1948, after Truman refused to allow Marriner Eccles to run for re-election as president, she chose to remain on the board for another three years, until 1951. Eccles later played a key role in facilitating the 1951 Federal Reserve-Treasury Agreement, which established the Federal Reserve's independence.

Many major historical turning points often involve unexpected figures who "acted cowardly their whole lives," only to suddenly undergo a complete personality change at the end, thus propelling the course of history.

02

The feud between US presidents and Federal Reserve chairmen is actually a tradition: for example, Lyndon Johnson vs. William Martin, Nixon vs. Arthur Burns, and Volcker vs. almost the entire American society.

However, the use of the Department of Justice for a criminal investigation is unprecedented in the 112-year history of the Federal Reserve.

Due to its shocking nature, the event prompted central bank governors from around the world to issue a joint letter of solidarity in support of Powell and protest against Trump's political attacks. It evoked a strong sense of justice.

This event is a historic moment in the beacon of the United States, but not in other "non-beacon" countries around the world. In 2023, Polish President Donald Tusk came to power, and one of his campaign promises was to remove Adam Glapiński, the governor of the National Bank of Poland, accusing him of politicizing monetary policy and favoring the previous government. Immediately after being elected, less than a year later, the Polish government subpoenaed Grapinski, accusing him of engaging in illegal public finance operations, constituting a crime. Grapinski's response was not to release a video, but to appeal to the European Central Bank (ECB). ECB President Lagarde immediately wrote a formal letter in support of Grapinski, warning the Polish authorities against taking illegal actions. Here's the key point: In this incident, the global central bank circle, at least the European Central Bank circle, did not stand on the side of the "victim," nor did it collectively voice its support as it did for Powell. The matter was left unresolved. This is interesting: Is the "independence" card something that varies from person to person (or country to country)? I'm not an expert on Eastern European issues, so there are certainly many details I haven't covered. But this contrast itself is worth pondering. Central bank governors are accustomed to viewing themselves as technocrats, transcending partisan politics, neither left nor right, and only recognizing data. I wonder if you've ever felt this way (I have)—whenever I hear the word "independence," I can't help but feel a sense of awe, as if this basic requirement places them on a moral high ground. But this self-perception overlooks a growing change: technocratic governance itself, in many people's eyes, is no longer a neutral and objective entity—because "neutrality" itself is also a political stance—at least from the perspective of the so-called populist wave, "technocrats" may precisely represent what they are rebelling against: a group of elites making decisions for the people behind the scenes. In this context, the defense of technocratic governance and institutional independence is no longer neutral. You may think you're above politics, but to others, you are part of it. What will happen next? My first thought was—the Fed Chair AI prediction competition is becoming more suspenseful and unpredictable (the competition introduction is here: "Two Kevins Run Fast: Human Intuition vs. AI Intelligence Analysis vs. Market Collective Wisdom"; the progress is here: "Fed Chair Prediction Competition Update 1: Can AI Find the 'Behind-the-Scenes Manipulator'?")—and that's exactly what I wanted—variables, new situations, and new narratives are the best opportunities to demonstrate the value of AI in "discovering narratives." The selection of the next Federal Reserve Chairman has thus become more uncertain. The probability of Powell deciding to remain on the board and stand up to Trump is already a new disruptive factor, and changes in the board will also affect the final nomination. Meanwhile, external influencing factors are also beginning to change. A Republican senator (North Carolina Senator Thom Tillis) has already issued a statement indicating that he will oppose any Trump-nominated Fed candidate, including Powell's successor, until the legal issues are resolved. A second possible impact is that being a civil servant in the United States has become a high-risk profession. Truly capable and insightful people might consider entering politics again and again. The logic is very simple, and you can probably guess: If I offend Trump and get a summons—I'll have to spend a fortune on lawyers and waste time and energy on litigation. I might never be able to work normally again. This is the "chilling effect": when the government uses all its power against an individual, "retaliating" becomes an extreme luxury. Third, and perhaps most importantly: what about the market? When the event first occurred, the market reaction was quite mild, and long-term US Treasury yields did not plummet. Did we overreact, and that capital wasn't worried? Not necessarily. If we were to give this event a name, "institutional rupture" wouldn't be an exaggeration. The process of repricing the global financial credit system is somewhat like bankruptcy—a slow collapse, then a sudden crash. As long as the Federal Reserve Board retains some restraining power, the market reaction may not be too significant; but once a certain critical point is reached, change can happen "overnight, in an instant." Thinking further ahead: the very foundation of the US dollar could be weakened as a result. It's well known that the Federal Reserve's power extends beyond setting interest rates; it also controls the financial system's water, electricity, and networks (the dollar payment system and financial infrastructure). However, this instability in the foundation won't be reflected in Treasury yields.

data.title: Cryptocurrency will become a daily payment tool for Russian residents amid Western containment.
data.description:
As the West tightens its financial blockade against Russia, cryptocurrency, originally seen as a "decentralized experiment," has been thrust into the core of Russia's countermeasures. It wasn't "chosen," but rather forced to take center stage as traditional channels became increasingly ineffective. From redefining it at the legislative level to the actual entry of banks and exchanges, and the coordinated implementation of taxation, mining, and cross-border settlement systems, Russia is transforming cryptocurrency from a passively tolerated, marginal option into a financial tool that can be institutionally controlled. For Russia, sanctions are no longer just pressure, but a trigger to formally integrate cryptocurrency into the financial system.

 Encryption is no longer "special"

If we turn back the clock three years, Russia's attitude towards cryptocurrencies was still quite cautious.

In 2021, Russian President Vladimir Putin publicly acknowledged the value of cryptocurrencies but explicitly denied their applicability to oil trade.

In 2022, the Russian Ministry of Finance further prohibited the acceptance of Bitcoin for oil exports.

However, this stance was gradually overturned by reality over the following three years.

In March 2025, according to informed sources, Russia had begun using cryptocurrencies as a settlement tool in its oil trade with India to circumvent Western sanctions.

On January 14, 2026, this change was formally incorporated into policy. The Chairman of the Financial Market Committee of the Russian State Duma publicly confirmed that a bill to remove cryptocurrencies from the "special financial supervision" framework has been prepared. This may promote the widespread adoption of cryptocurrencies in the lives of Russian residents in the future. According to the proposed plan: Ordinary residents can participate in cryptocurrency asset allocation (with a set amount limit) Professional institutions will no longer be restricted Cryptocurrencies will be allowed for international settlement Related assets can enter other countries' markets after being issued in Russia This is not a "loosening of regulations," but rather a redefinition of the position of cryptocurrencies in the financial system.

  

Over the past year, Russia has faced a set of highly realistic constraints:

  • Russian legal entities and individuals continue to face restrictions on using traditional currencies for overseas payments
  • Decreased efficiency and unstable pathways in foreign trade settlements
  • Financial institutions have been forced to seek alternative settlement methods

Against this backdrop, the Central Bank of Russia has acknowledged in official statements that:

Relaxing cryptocurrency circulation rules is a necessary choice under these realistic constraints.

data.title: Where are the profit opportunities in the crypto market in 2026?
data.description: This is a list of ten core predictions for the crypto market in 2026, covering important topics such as Bitcoin price outlook, mainstream altcoin narratives, and the prospects for the integration of crypto and artificial intelligence. The content is presented by renowned analyst Miles Deutscher and is part of his annual strategy analysis, used to effectively clarify the direction of investment in the new year. Even if you don't completely agree with all the views in this article, it is hoped that it will inspire you to think, help you build your own predictive framework, and prepare you to seize the biggest upward opportunities of the year. 10. Predicting a 5x Increase in Market Trading Volume Since January/February 2025, predicted market trading volume has increased approximately tenfold. Miles believes this trend will continue, predicting that by 2026, at least one month's trading volume will reach five times the current level. Based on current figures, a 5x increase would translate to approximately $95 billion in monthly trading volume. Another key driver is the rise of derivative protocols built around prediction markets (the first leveraged prediction market is under development), which will further amplify trading volume. 9. Explosive Growth in Perpetual Contract Trading Miles' core logic is: Perpetual contracts will continue to penetrate mainstream asset classes, no longer limited to crypto token trading. By 2025, perpetual contract technology based on crypto infrastructure will achieve breakthroughs in instant settlement, user experience, and decentralization. Secondly, gold, metals, and stocks are in a strong bull market cycle. Historical experience shows that when market cycles near their peak, investors tend to move further down the risk curve in pursuit of returns (perpetual contracts will benefit along with small-cap stocks/emerging markets). Furthermore, in many scenarios, accessing these assets through crypto channels is far more convenient and efficient than through the traditional financial system. Based on these multiple factors, Miles predicts an explosive growth in stock/metal trading volume on decentralized perpetual contract exchanges, with perpetual contract trading categories far exceeding the scope of crypto tokens. 8. The ICO Revival Continues Significant changes in the regulatory environment have unprecedentedly broadened the channels for public participation in token sales. Current ICO participation is at an all-time high, and Miles expects this upward trend to continue. 7. Altcoins Favor Stronger Profit Models The market trend has clearly shifted towards favoring projects with real business potential, rather than purely speculative targets. Of course, speculation will always exist, but more and more investors (regardless of size) are searching for the intrinsic value that truly supports token value amidst the dilution of abundant liquidity. Miles believes that protocols with real profitability will continue to dominate. In short, protocols with actual revenue mechanisms will be more sustainable than projects without substantial returns. Focus on companies/projects/teams that truly generate revenue. 6. RWA to Experience Second-Best Year in History Miles believes RWA (Real-World Assets) will have its best year ever, but technically, surpassing the $14 billion growth target of 2025 will be challenging. Liquidity is expected to continue flowing into the sector, maintaining high growth, but the percentage growth rate may be lower than in 2025. A large number of yield generation/asset tokenization opportunities will emerge in 2026, offering a wide range of altcoin trading options. 5. Digital Banking Sector Sees Fastest Growth Crypto asset/stablecoin banking seems to have finally reached a turning point. Infrastructure has gradually matched market demand, driving explosive growth in many services. The ability to easily convert between crypto assets and fiat currency is a solution urgently needed by the market. This sector boasts the largest reachable market (almost the entire financial world) and is actively addressing many pain points in developing countries and regions underserved by traditional finance. This is a massive market, and Miles anticipates a true breakthrough in 2026. 4. AI and AI Agents Return to Their Essence Last January, the frenzied AI hype drove up the price of crypto-AI fusion protocols, but the technology was not yet mature. Entering 2026, the situation is different; the technology finally meets market expectations. Miles believes that 2026 will undoubtedly be a pivotal year for AI development (although it is every year), and retail investors' speculative enthusiasm for AI's potential could easily spill over into the crypto space. The convergence of crypto and AI has a perfect synergy: Crypto provides freedom in financial channels; AI brings automation capabilities, representing the future of finance. This is expected to spawn opportunities in multiple AI sub-sectors, including x402, bots, agent workflows, and AI data/infrastructure. 3. Stablecoin Supply Grows Over 50% Last year, the total supply of stablecoins increased by 50% (from $200 billion to $300 billion). Catalyzed by the US GENIUS Act (a stablecoin regulatory act), Miles expects a similar increase this year. 2. Institutions Will Become the Dominant Force in the Crypto Market This cycle has been driven by institutional forces (such as digital asset trusts and ETF development). Miles expects this institutionally dominated market structure to continue to deepen. Investors should focus on tokens/protocols that can attract institutional interest (returning to the core view of focusing on projects with actual profitability). 1. Bitcoin's year-end closing price will be higher than the beginning of the year. Whether Bitcoin will experience a crazy surge to over $150,000 this year depends on multiple factors: Capital inflows/buyer demand; Digital asset trusts; Macroeconomic background. Miles believes that while it's impossible to predict whether all positive factors will trigger extreme market movements, he firmly believes that Bitcoin will close with a positive annual candle in 2026. This means BTC needs to close above the $90,000 range. Miles' bullish logic for predicting a year-end rally is based on the following: We are in the final year of the business cycle. The current price bottoming pattern is similar to previous years.
